rulings.Case Title: JAMES STUART ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 8, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant The Wm. Powell Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30a.m., not 9:30a.m., Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARTER ET AL VS. SOCO WEST, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Honeywell International Inc., Fka Alliedsignal Inc., Successor-In-Interest To The Bendix Corporations Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 8, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Honeywell International, Inc.'s motion for change of venue is denied. Defendant failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice will be promoted by transfer. (C.C.P. § 397(c).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30a.m., not 9:30a.m., Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARTER ET AL VS. SOCO WEST, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Honeywell Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 8, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Clayton Industries' joinder in Honeywell International, Inc.'s motion for change of venue is denied. Defendant failed to provide the Court with a courtesy copy of its joinder. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30a.m., not 9:30a.m., Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
